title: Secret Storage Backend
content: AES-256-GCM encrypted file-based storage with master key derived from operator passphrase. Architecture-only.

title: Encryption at Rest
content: All credential values encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM. Master key never stored in plaintext.

title: Encryption in Transit
content: TLS 1.2+ required for all credential access. Certificate pinning recommended.

title: Access Control
content: Role-based access: operator (read), reviewer (read), admin (read/write). No anonymous access.

title: Operator Identity
content: Operator must authenticate with MFA before accessing credentials. Identity logged.

title: Reviewer Identity
content: Reviewer must authenticate independently. Cannot be same person as operator.

title: Least Privilege
content: Each credential set scoped to minimum required permissions. No blanket access.

title: Read-Only Key Class
content: Separate key class for balance/position reads. Cannot submit orders.

title: Trade Key Class
content: Trade-scoped key class. Can submit/cancel orders. Cannot withdraw.

title: Withdraw Permission Forbidden
content: Withdraw permission must be explicitly forbidden on all keys. Any key with withdraw permission must be revoked immediately.

title: Rotation Policy
content: Keys rotated every 90 days. Rotation requires human approval. Old keys revoked after rotation.

title: Revocation Policy
content: Emergency revocation available 24/7. Revocation logged and requires post-incident review.

title: Redaction Policy
content: All credential values redacted in logs, reports, and outputs. Only last 4 chars visible.

title: Audit Trail
content: Every credential access logged with timestamp, operator, action, and result. Tamper-evident.

title: Incident Response
content: Credential compromise triggers immediate revocation, audit review, and stakeholder notification.

title: Environment Isolation
content: Testnet and production credentials stored in separate vaults. No cross-environment access.

title: Manual Approval Dependency
content: Credential creation, rotation, and revocation require human approval. No automated credential lifecycle.
